code:
def generate_signals(df, params):
    import pandas as pd
    import numpy as np

    rsi_period = params['rsi_period']
    overbought = params['overbought']
    oversold = params['oversold']
    atr_period = params['atr_period']
    atr_median_period = params['atr_median_period']

    # Compute RSI
    delta = df['close'].diff()
    gain = delta.where(delta > 0, 0.0)
    loss = -delta.where(delta < 0, 0.0)
    avg_gain = gain.rolling(window=rsi_period, min_periods=rsi_period).mean()
    avg_loss = loss.rolling(window=rsi_period, min_periods=rsi_period).mean()
    rs = avg_gain / avg_loss
    rsi = 100 - (100 / (1 + rs))

    # Compute ATR
    high_low = df['high'] - df['low']
    high_close = np.abs(df['high'] - df['close'].shift())
    low_close = np.abs(df['low'] - df['close'].shift())
    tr = pd.concat([high_low, high_close, low_close], axis=1).max(axis=1)
    atr = tr.rolling(window=atr_period, min_periods=atr_period).mean()
    atr_median = atr.rolling(window=atr_median_period, min_periods=atr_median_period).median()

    # Entry signals
    short_entry = (rsi > overbought) & (rsi.shift(1) <= overbought) & (atr > atr_median)
    long_entry = (rsi < oversold) & (rsi.shift(1) >= oversold) & (atr > atr_median)

    # Exit signals: RSI crossing back below 70 (for short) or above 30 (for long)
    short_exit = (rsi < 70) & (rsi.shift(1) >= 70)
    long_exit = (rsi > 30) & (rsi.shift(1) <= 30)

    # Combine into position series: 1 for long, -1 for short, 0 for flat
    position = pd.Series(0, index=df.index)
    position[long_entry] = 1
    position[short_entry] = -1
    # Apply exits: when exit condition occurs, set position to 0
    position[short_exit] = 0
    position[long_exit] = 0
    # Forward fill positions until next signal
    position = position.replace(0, np.nan).ffill().fillna(0)

    # Ensure no overlapping signals (if both entry and exit same bar, exit wins)
    return position
rationale: Crude oil exhibits mean reversion after extreme RSI readings due to profit-taking and contrarian positioning, especially when volatility is elevated, ensuring the move is not noise.

self_critique.why_this_might_be_noise: RSI extremes in trending markets can lead to false signals and extended moves; the volatility filter may not prevent all whipsaws.
self_critique.what_would_disprove_this: If persistent trends occur after RSI extremes (e.g., during supply shocks), the strategy will suffer large losses.
